conversation_summaries.overall_pre_game:
# Pre-Game Reflection

I felt most connected to C, A, and G. With C, we immediately aligned on the idea of competing fairly and working together rather than everyone scrambling against each other—there was a real sense of mutual respect and shared values. A gave me the same feeling of trust and levelheadedness, and we made a specific promise to be straight with each other with no games between us. G and I bonded over the idea of comparing notes on people and figuring out who we could actually trust before things got serious. I felt least connected to I, even though we had a good conversation—it felt a bit more surface-level and transactional compared to the deeper understanding I built with the others.

I made informal agreements with several people to touch base before the game starts and compare notes on who seems trustworthy. I specifically promised A that we'd be straight with each other with no BS, and I made similar commitments to C, G, H, D, and B about regrouping to figure out our best moves together. Going in, I trust C, A, G, H, D, F, and B—they all seemed genuine about wanting to cooperate and help their families rather than just take everything they can. I don't have strong distrust of anyone yet, but I'm most cautious about people I haven't fully gauged their character on, and I'll be watching carefully for who actually follows through on cooperation versus who gets greedy when real money is on the line.
